enterpriseWatchlist.notes: LPU inference chips, fastest LLM serving

enterpriseWatchlist.name: Modular/Mojo
enterpriseWatchlist.domain: AI Infrastructure (CUDA alternative, MAX AI platform / Mojo language) — Acquired by Qualcomm
enterpriseWatchlist.stage: Acquired by Qualcomm for ~$4B all-stock (announced June 24, 2026; pending regulatory approval, expected H2 2026 close)
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: Qualcomm agreed to acquire Modular (founded by Chris Lattner + Tim Davis, ex-Google/Apple/LLVM) for ~$4B all-stock on June 24, 2026. Strategic rationale: Qualcomm gains Modular's MAX AI platform and Mojo language — the most credible hardware-agnostic ML execution layer built to run across NVIDIA, AMD, Intel, and Qualcomm chips. Direct CUDA alternative: MAX AI already runs PyTorch and TensorFlow graphs on non-NVIDIA hardware without code changes. Post-acquisition, Qualcomm's Oryon/Hexagon NPU ecosystem gains a first-class ML runtime, enabling on-device inference at competitive parity with CoreML and TensorRT. The $4B price for a compiler-runtime company signals that the ML execution layer is now viewed as a strategic moat, not a commodity. For engineers: if MAX AI ships as a free Qualcomm SDK, the CUDA migration TAM shrinks significantly — hardware-agnostic ML deployment becomes the new standard.

enterpriseWatchlist.name: Together AI
enterpriseWatchlist.stage: Series C ($8.3B valuation, $800M raised July 1, 2026; Aramco Ventures + NVIDIA + Vista Equity + General Catalyst led)
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: $800M Series C at $8.3B valuation (July 1, 2026) — 2.5× Series B in 16 months. $1.15B+ annual bookings; open-source model usage tripled YoY. 500 MW compute committed independently by investors (CoreWeave-style infrastructure behind the API). Aramco Ventures leading + NVIDIA strategic co-investment signals open-source cloud is becoming a key GPU distribution channel at hyperscaler margins. Serves Llama, DeepSeek, Qwen, Mixtral at $0.60–1.20/M tokens (vs $15/M for Claude Sonnet) — 10–25× cost gap pays for enterprise migration in weeks. Primary competitors: DeepInfra, Fireworks AI, Replicate. Together.ai growing toward hyperscaler-scale training + inference at commercial speed.

enterpriseWatchlist.name: Cerebras
enterpriseWatchlist.stage: Public (CBRS on Nasdaq; IPO priced May 13, 2026 at $185/share; $5.55B raised; $56.4B fully diluted valuation; began trading May 14, 2026 with early prints implying ~$100B market cap)
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: WSE-3 wafer-scale chip; $510M 2025 revenue; $10B OpenAI compute deal; priced at $185 (well above $150-160 revised range); raised $5.55B — largest AI chip IPO in history; Day 1 trading (May 14) showed early prints near $350/share (~$100B implied market cap); validates pure-play AI infrastructure as public market category; NVIDIA alternatives now investor-credible at scale

enterpriseWatchlist.name: Baseten
enterpriseWatchlist.stage: Series D ($2.15B valuation)
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: Serverless ML model deployment; $150M Series D Apr 2026; abstracts GPU fleet for enterprise inference workloads; competes with Modal Labs, Parasail

enterpriseWatchlist.name: Cursor
enterpriseWatchlist.stage: Acquired by SpaceX/xAI for $60B all-stock (announced June 16, 2026; expected Q3 2026 close)
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: SpaceX exercised its option to acquire Anysphere (Cursor) for $60B in all-stock — the largest AI developer tools M&A in history — 10 days after SpaceX's own Nasdaq IPO. Deal rationale: Cursor's 150M lines/day of enterprise code trains Grok's coding capabilities; Cursor gains access to Colossus (200K H100 supercluster) for model training. 50K+ enterprise clients (~2/3 of Fortune 500). SpaceX (merged with xAI) now has Grok model + Cursor IDE + Colossus compute = vertically integrated AI developer platform competing with Anthropic/OpenAI. Enterprise risk: clients may face pressure to use Grok over Claude/GPT within the IDE. GOOGL fell 5% on news; SpaceX shares rose 8% (market cap ~$2.7T, surpassing Amazon). Enterprise engineering teams should audit Cursor vendor dependency before Q3 close.

enterpriseWatchlist.name: MatX
enterpriseWatchlist.stage: Series B ($500M, Feb 2026)
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: Ex-Google TPU founders; $500M Series B led by Jane Street + Situational Awareness (Leopold Aschenbrenner); targeting 10x LLM training perf over H100; TSMC-fabbed, first shipments 2027; credible non-NVIDIA training chip challenger

enterpriseWatchlist.name: Lambda Labs
enterpriseWatchlist.stage: Series E ($1.5B, Nov 2025) + $1B credit facility (May 2026)
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: 'Superintelligence Cloud' positioning; $2.5B+ total capital deployed to build gigawatt-scale AI factories (10K Blackwell Ultra GPUs in KC facility); J.P. Morgan-led $1B credit facility announced May 7, 2026; competes with CoreWeave (CRWV, IPO'd) for GPU-native cloud market below hyperscalers

enterpriseWatchlist.name: Project Prometheus
enterpriseWatchlist.domain: Physical AI for Industrial Manufacturing Simulation
enterpriseWatchlist.stage: $10B round at $38B valuation (closed Apr 2026, JPMorgan + BlackRock co-led)
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: Co-CEOs Jeff Bezos + Vik Bajaj; builds AI simulation models to automate chip fabs, automotive assembly, and aerospace manufacturing; launched with $6.2B, added $10B round at $38B val in Apr 2026; Bezos also targeting $100B acquisition fund for industrial companies; bifurcates from 'embodied robotics' into factory process simulation — $16T manufacturing TAM with <2% AI penetration

enterpriseWatchlist.name: Sunday Robotics
enterpriseWatchlist.stage: Series B ($165M, $1.15B valuation, May 2026)
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: Building 'Memo', a household robot entering beta later in 2026; $165M Series B at $1.15B valuation — first consumer robotics unicorn in this category; targets sub-$5K price point; competes with labor costs (cleaning services, elder care); Mountain View, CA. Category signal: if Memo ships, it validates consumer robotics as mass market (analogous to Roomba → iPhone step change)

enterpriseWatchlist.name: Nous Research / Hermes Agent
enterpriseWatchlist.stage: Open Source / Venture-backed research lab
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: Hermes Agent reached 160K+ GitHub stars in May 2026 -- fastest-growing open-source agent framework of the year, beating OpenClaw's star velocity at equivalent age. Key innovation: persistent cross-session memory + automatic skill file generation from successful task completions. Agents accumulate proprietary workflow knowledge as organizational IP. Distinct from orchestration-layer agents (LangGraph, CrewAI): this is the memory + self-improvement persistence layer. Signal: enterprise dev tooling adoption velocity indicates the 'agent memory' infrastructure layer is becoming a standard requirement for production agents.

enterpriseWatchlist.name: Skild AI
enterpriseWatchlist.stage: Growth (acquired Zebra Technologies Robotics Automation, Apr 2026)
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: Acquired Zebra Technologies' Robotics Automation business (Symmetry platform + Fetch Robotics AMRs) in April 2026 to create the first end-to-end AI-native warehouse automation stack. Skild's omni-bodied foundation model controls humanoids, robotic dogs, arms, and AMRs from a single AI brain -- no per-robot reprogramming. Zebra received cash + equity stake. Positions Skild as the full-stack robotics vendor for logistics: hardware-agnostic AI orchestration across all warehouse robot form factors. Category signal: physical AI for logistics is consolidating from specialized to generalist foundation models.

enterpriseWatchlist.name: Rhoda AI
enterpriseWatchlist.stage: Series A ($450M, $1.7B valuation, Mar 2026)
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: Exited stealth March 2026 with $450M Series A (Khosla, Mayfield, Temasek, John Doerr) to build FutureVision -- a Direct Video Action (DVA) model pre-trained on hundreds of millions of internet videos. Rather than teleoperated trajectory data, Rhoda trains on video to learn physics, motion, and physical interaction priors, then post-trains on small amounts of robot-specific data. The DVA model continuously observes environment, predicts future states as video, converts predictions to robot actions every few hundred milliseconds in a closed loop. Key differentiation: generalization across novel environments without re-training. Watch: DVA architecture could be the foundation model approach that finally bridges the robotics reality gap.

enterpriseWatchlist.name: Nscale
enterpriseWatchlist.stage: Series C ($2B, $14.6B valuation, Mar 2026) + $900M revolving credit facility (July 7, 2026; 12-bank syndicate: JPMorgan, Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, MUFG, Bank of America, Deutsche Bank, Mizuho + 5 others)
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: Europe's largest-ever Series C (March 9, 2026), 4× valuation jump from Series B. UK/Norway-headquartered AI compute hyperscaler backed by NVIDIA, Dell, Nokia, Citadel, Jane Street, and Point72. Nick Clegg (ex-Meta VP Global Affairs, ex-EU Deputy PM) and Sheryl Sandberg join board — EU regulatory fluency as a core product differentiator. Targets regulated EU enterprises (banks, hospitals, defense) blocked from US-CLOUD-Act-governed hyperscalers by GDPR Art. 44, EU AI Act, and NIS2. July 7, 2026 update: closed $900M revolving credit facility syndicated across 12 major banks (JPMorgan, Goldman, Morgan Stanley, MUFG, BofA, Deutsche, Mizuho + 5 others) — total capital now exceeds $2.9B. The 12-bank syndicate means lenders are now pricing Nscale as infrastructure company risk, not startup risk. Credit facility earmarked for US + Europe + APAC data center expansion. Full-stack sovereign ownership (energy + data centers + GPU compute + software) now at the scale where it can credibly compete with CoreWeave and Lambda for mid-market GPU cloud deals outside EU.

enterpriseWatchlist.name: Anthropic
enterpriseWatchlist.domain: Frontier AI Model Platform (Claude API / Enterprise AI)
enterpriseWatchlist.stage: Pre-IPO (Confidential S-1 filed June 1, 2026; $965B valuation post $65B Series H; Nasdaq Oct 2026 target)
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: Confidentially filed S-1 with SEC on June 1, 2026 after closing $65B Series H led by Altimeter, Sequoia, Dragoneer. $965B post-money valuation — world's most valuable private AI company, eclipsing OpenAI's $852B for the first time. $47B ARR run-rate as of May 2026 (up from $30B in April 2026); first profitable quarter projected Q2 2026. Oct 2026 Nasdaq listing targeting $1T+ debut valuation. Enterprise signal: 72 open sales roles vs 67 research roles in the S-1 — Claude is now primarily a commercial enterprise utility. Expected to beat OpenAI to public markets by 6-12 months. Key risk for enterprise CTOs: once public, pricing power hardens — companies with >$5M annual Claude API spend face accelerating lock-in.

enterpriseWatchlist.name: NEURA Robotics
enterpriseWatchlist.domain: Full-Stack Physical AI Platform (Humanoid + Mobile Robots + Cobot Arms)
enterpriseWatchlist.stage: Series C ($1.4B, June 10, 2026; investors: NVIDIA, Amazon, Qualcomm, Tether, Bosch, Schaeffler, European Investment Bank)
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: Largest full-stack robotics funding round in history (June 10, 2026). Germany-based physical AI platform building humanoid robots, mobile robots, light robot arms, and sensor kits for manufacturing + supply chain. Key moat: 'NEURA Gyms' — real-world proprietary training environments for cognitive robots, making experiential training data the structural advantage analogous to Waymo's miles-driven dataset. Investor coalition (NVIDIA silicon + Amazon cloud/distribution + Qualcomm edge inference + Bosch/Schaeffler OEM customers) creates a vertically integrated route to European industrial market. EU domicile is a product feature for GDPR/NIS2-regulated European enterprise customers blocked from US-cloud-governed robots. Target: serial production to multi-million robots by 2030.

enterpriseWatchlist.name: Armada
enterpriseWatchlist.domain: Edge AI Infrastructure (Disconnected / Remote / Military AI Compute)
enterpriseWatchlist.stage: Series B ($230M, $2.2B valuation; led by 8090 Industries, BlackRock, Overmatch Ventures)
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: Builds purpose-built AI infrastructure nodes for edge environments where centralized GPU cloud is unavailable: military forward operating bases, remote industrial sites, maritime/airborne platforms. $230M Series B led by BlackRock + 8090 Industries (defense tech-focused) + Overmatch Ventures. Distinct from Lambda Labs / CoreWeave (centralized AI factories) — Armada targets constrained-compute environments where latency, air-gap, and sovereignty requirements make cloud inference impossible. Signal: defense AI and remote industrial AI inference is large enough to attract $2.2B valuations — suggests the 'last-mile AI compute' problem is now funded at infrastructure scale, not just as military R&D.

enterpriseWatchlist.name: DeepInfra
enterpriseWatchlist.domain: Open-Source Inference API Gateway & Model Aggregation
enterpriseWatchlist.stage: Series B ($107M, May 4, 2026; co-led by 500 Global + Georges Harik; investors: NVIDIA, Samsung Next, Supermicro, Felicis, A.Capital, Peak6, Upper90)
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: Processes 5 trillion tokens/week via 190+ open-source models (Llama, Mixtral, Qwen, Phi, Gemma, DeepSeek) behind a single OpenAI-compatible API endpoint. 25× token volume growth since Series A; revenue 3× in 2026. NVIDIA's strategic co-investment signals DeepInfra is a key open-source compute distribution channel — buying GPU time at scale without hyperscaler markup. Category thesis: the OpenAI-compatible API spec is becoming the de facto standard for LLM routing; DeepInfra is building the provider-agnostic inference aggregation layer that lets enterprises switch models without code changes. At current growth trajectory, the inference gateway category is inflecting from startup niche to infrastructure-tier.

enterpriseWatchlist.name: OpenAI
enterpriseWatchlist.domain: Frontier AI Lab (Model APIs, ChatGPT, Codex, ChatGPT Work super app)
enterpriseWatchlist.stage: Pre-IPO (Confidential S-1 filed June 8, 2026; Goldman Sachs + Morgan Stanley leading; $850B+ valuation; Q4 2026 Nasdaq target)
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: OpenAI filed a confidential S-1 with the SEC on June 8, 2026 — one week after Anthropic's filing — officially entering the IPO pipeline. At $850B+ valuation with $25B ARR (as of the filing), an OpenAI public debut would rank among the largest US IPOs in history. Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley are lead underwriters; Q4 2026 Nasdaq listing is targeted. July 10, 2026 update: launched ChatGPT Work — a 'super app' powered by GPT-5.6 that unifies ChatGPT + document creation + presentation generation + one-click hosted website publishing into a single product. Rolled out July 10 to Pro/Enterprise/Edu users first. Strategic significance: OpenAI is no longer an API provider — it now owns the full knowledge-work workflow layer, competing directly with Microsoft Office, Google Workspace, Notion, and Canva simultaneously. For engineering teams using OpenAI APIs: the super app creates a consumer-grade daily-workflow lock-in that API access alone never could — the moat is no longer model quality but accumulated work history and artifact ownership. Post-IPO API pricing will accelerate this trend.

enterpriseWatchlist.name: Agility Robotics
enterpriseWatchlist.stage: Pre-IPO SPAC (Churchill Capital XI merger announced June 24, 2026; $2.5B pre-money valuation; $620M raised; $200M PIPE led by Foxconn; ticker AGLT on Nasdaq, Q3 2026 close expected)
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: First pure-play US-listed humanoid robot company. Backed by Amazon + NVIDIA. Digit v5 has 65,000+ logged commercial operating hours across 9 enterprise facilities (GXO Logistics, Schaeffler, Toyota Manufacturing Canada) — separates Agility from render-ware demos. $300M+ in committed multi-year orders. Foxconn leading the $200M PIPE is the category-defining signal: world's largest contract manufacturer is betting humanoid robots enter its own production lines, validating a scale-manufacturing path at potentially <$50K unit cost. Cooperative-safety-first design (robots work beside people without isolation cages) sets a new enterprise deployment criterion. AGLT post-SPAC stock performance will be the benchmark institutional investors use to price all humanoid robotics companies (Figure AI, NEURA, Tesla Optimus, Apptronik). Watch Q3 2026 for SPAC close + first public earnings call as the category's first public data point on robotics unit economics.

enterpriseWatchlist.name: Ollama
enterpriseWatchlist.stage: Series B ($65M, Theory Ventures led, July 9, 2026)
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: Ollama raised a $65M Series B led by Theory Ventures on July 9, 2026 — the first institutional venture round for the most widely-used open-source local LLM inference runtime (160K+ GitHub stars, 1M+ monthly downloads). Ollama provides a Docker-like CLI interface (`ollama pull llama3`, `ollama run`) for running LLMs locally on Mac, Windows, and Linux with hardware-optimized backends. The Series B signals two things: (1) the 'run open-weight models on your laptop or private server' category is commercially viable — enterprises pay for Ollama Pro features (model management, fleet deployment, SLA support) even when the core runtime is free; (2) Theory Ventures (known for infrastructure bets: PlanetScale, Temporal, Retool) views local inference as infrastructure-tier, not consumer tool. For ML engineers: Ollama is the reference implementation for 'what does great local inference DX look like' — its model library, REST API (/api/generate), and pull/run CLI design patterns are being copied by enterprise tools. Signal for personalized AI: on-device inference for privacy-sensitive personalization (health, finance, legal) is now infrastructure-grade, not experimental.

enterpriseWatchlist.name: Odyssey
enterpriseWatchlist.domain: AI World Simulation Models (Physical AI Training Data Infrastructure)
enterpriseWatchlist.stage: Series B ($310M, $1.45B valuation, June 17, 2026; Natural Capital led; Amazon, GV, AMD Ventures, IQT, EQT co-investors)
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: World simulation model company founded by AV pioneers Oliver Cameron + Jeff Hawke. AWS preferred cloud + Amazon Trainium chips for training — positions Odyssey as Amazon's world-model answer to NVIDIA Cosmos 3. Model portfolio: Odyssey-2 Max (physics-accurate world simulation), Starchild-1 (real-time multimodal world model), Agora-1 (multi-agent interaction), PROWL (active exploration via self-supervised data generation). Amazon's strategic co-investment + Jeff Dean (Google chief scientist) as individual backer signals this is foundational infrastructure for physical AI training, not a niche robotics tool. Enterprise signal: world simulation models are commoditizing the 'synthetic training data' infrastructure layer — custom physics simulation pipelines (Gazebo, Isaac Sim) face stranded asset risk in 12-18 months. Watch for AWS SageMaker integration that lets robotics teams access Odyssey world models as managed training data APIs.

enterpriseWatchlist.name: Fireworks AI
enterpriseWatchlist.domain: Specialized LLM Inference API Platform (Open-Source Model Serving at Hyperscale)
enterpriseWatchlist.stage: Series D ($1.505B, $17.5B valuation, July 16, 2026; led by Atreides Management, Index Ventures, TCV; NVIDIA co-investor)
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: Fireworks AI closed a $1.505B Series D at $17.5B valuation on July 16, 2026 — the largest single round in dedicated open-source LLM inference infrastructure. $1B+ ARR reached; daily token volume grew from 15T to 40T tokens/day in the past 6 months. Enterprise customers include Uber, Shopify, Doximity, Elastic, GitLab, MongoDB. Core thesis: enterprises need a third option between build-your-own GPU cluster and paying hyperscaler (OpenAI/Anthropic) rates — a specialized inference layer that serves any open-weight model (Llama, Mistral, Qwen, DeepSeek) at hyperscaler throughput with SLAs, at 10–25x lower cost per token than closed APIs. The $1.5B raise will expand GPU capacity and fund a 'Specialized Intelligence' positioning (fine-tuned models + compound AI systems, not just generic inference). NVIDIA's co-investment signals open-source inference platforms are now a primary GPU distribution channel alongside hyperscalers. For ML engineers: Fireworks' function-calling, JSON mode, and structured outputs are the reference implementation for production-grade open-model inference APIs — their latency and throughput benchmarks set the comparison baseline for enterprise open-model deployments.
